title: T-127 — Authorization Policies screen (SET-011b)
prompt:
# T-127 — Authorization Policies screen (SET-011b)

## Goal
Build UX SET-011b Authorization Policies as the Settings screen for configuring per-org authorization policies that gate NPD post-release edits and Technical product-spec approval.

Canonical Settings prototype source of truth is `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/*.jsx`. No exact canonical prototype exists for SET-011b, so implement spec-driven from `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md SET-011b` and reuse available card/form patterns only where they match the UX contract.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/authorization/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/authorization/page.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given `/settings/authorization` renders, then it follows UX SET-011b: two policy cards, NPD post-release edit authorization and Technical product-spec approval gate, with read-only permission strings, role selectors, status badges, invariant banners, loading, missing-seed, misconfigured, and permission-denied states.
- Given a user without `settings.authorization.edit` views the screen, then write controls are hidden/disabled and the policy summary remains readable.
- Given policies are saved, when validation passes, then T-126 actions are called with an audit reason and the UI shows updated version/status; blockers are displayed without local-only bypass.
- Given Technical approval is required, then the gate rule code `technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1` is visible/read-only and factory-use blocking remains locked on.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: RTL page tests for the two policy cards, read-only vs edit permissions, missing seed, misconfigured blockers, audit reason required, save/discard, and audit-log link.
- GREEN: build screen wired to T-126 actions and policy summaries.
- Run page test, typecheck, lint, and capture UI evidence per policy.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement NPD or Technical application workflows in this Settings screen.
- Do not allow UI-only validation to bypass T-126 helper blockers.
- Do not allow `requires_new_version=false`, self-authorization defaults, or unlocked factory-use approval.
- Do not claim 1:1 prototype parity; exact canonical prototype is missing and UX SET-011b is authoritative.
## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED -> GREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC: pass/fail)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

## Prototype reference
**Source:** `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/access-screens.jsx:154-239`
**Label:** `security_screen`
**Component type:** form

> **No exact prototype** — closest visual pattern: `security_screen` at `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/access-screens.jsx:154-239`. Build in the same structural style as this screen. Re-use the Security screen's Section + SRow form pattern; render Authorization Policy rules as a list of toggles/selects with the same density and hint-text styling as 'Enforce 2FA' / 'Allowed methods' rows.

### Parity requirements (mandatory ACs)
The production implementation MUST match the prototype on three dimensions — add these as explicit ACs:
1. **Structural parity** — same sections, same field labels, same shadcn primitives invoked, same modals triggered from same actions.
2. **Visual parity** — same density tokens, same shadcn/Radix primitives (no drift to plain HTML), same layout proportions.
3. **Interaction parity** — same enable/disable rules, same loading/empty/error states, same keyboard focus order.

### Translation notes (from prototype-labels index)
- Section + SRow primitives → shadcn Card sections with label/hint pattern using FormDescription
- Toggle (2FA, SSO, SCIM) → shadcn Switch; each toggle triggers Server Action for security_settings upsert with RBAC Admin guard
- Checkbox group for allowed 2FA methods → shadcn Checkbox with useForm + zodResolver
- Password policy selects and number inputs → useForm with zod schema; Server Action for password_policy upsert
- Session settings selects → Server Action for session_policy upsert; values stored in security_config table
- Audit log table → Drizzle query on audit_log table with pagination; full log link routes to dedicated audit-log screen
- Hardcoded log entries → real audit_log rows from DB
- Hardcoded labels → next-intl keys

### shadcn primitives to use
`Switch`, `Checkbox`, `Select`, `Input`, `Table`, `Button`, `Badge`, `Card`

pipeline_inputs.details:
T-127 — Authorization Policies screen (SET-011b)

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Targeted tests and closeout evidence are provided.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Authorization Policies screen renders UX SET-011b two-card layout with NPD and Technical policy summaries, status badges, invariant banners and required states.
Users without settings.authorization.edit get a readable read-only screen with no save controls.
Save calls T-126 actions with audit reason; typed blockers render inline and cannot be bypassed client-side.
technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1 is visible/read-only and factory-use blocking remains locked on.

UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED: RTL tests for two policy cards, read-only/edit permissions, missing seed, misconfigured blockers, audit reason, save/discard and audit-log link.
GREEN: build screen wired to T-126 actions and policy summaries.
Run page test, typecheck and lint.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not implement NPD or Technical application workflows in this Settings screen.
Do not allow UI-only validation to bypass T-126 helper blockers.
Do not allow requires_new_version=false, self-authorization defaults or unlocked factory-use approval.
Do not claim 1:1 prototype parity; exact canonical prototype is missing and UX SET-011b is authoritative.
